data.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion T8 (LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe, OVERLAY).

VERDICT: T8 does not apply; no findings (empty checklist).

The T8 overlay is scoped to plans that define an LLM/agent system (prompts, sub-agents, reviewers, LLM output schemas, or LLM-consumed enums). This plan defines a deterministic, reducer-maintained provenance set (`managed_refs`) and a pure decision helper (`should_propagate_removal`) — there is no LLM/agent surface. Tool-grounded: no prompt/sub-agent/reviewer/LLM-schema construct appears in the reconciler tree beyond unrelated interactive-CLI confirmation prompts (acli.py, acli_graph.py); `managed_refs` itself has zero matches (parent builds it).

Even mapping the overlay's checks onto the plan's code-level constructs, it PASSES on every probe:
- (a) Value vocabulary defined: the only enum (`kind in {parent, blocks, depends_on, relates_to}`) is explicitly enumerated in What/AC1.
- (b) Decision rule co-located with its schema: `should_propagate_removal(ref, managed_refs)` is defined alongside the `managed_refs` field it reads.
- (c) State increment placement unambiguous: the monotonic fold is pinned to process_create (initial parent+deps), process_link, and parent-EDIT (incl. inbound-adopted), and is explicitly never reduced by UNLINK/detach.
- (d) Fallback for incomplete/failed sub-step specified: missing/absent `managed_refs` defaults to EMPTY ⇒ gate returns False (safe additive-only no-op; also the documented back-out posture).
- (e) No instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern; referenced constructs are fully specified.

Affirmations (T8 checklist, by analogy on the code-level constructs): no undefined-but-referenced enum; decision rule co-located with its schema; no ambiguously-placed state increment; failed/incomplete-substep fallback specified; referenced constructs exist and are fully specified (tool-verified).
